label: 생성
prompt:
GOVERNING: A single decisive moment of public contrition at the May 18th National Cemetery in Gwangju, South Korea, where a school-baseball-uniformed student bows his head low before the memorial tower as an elder's wrinkled hand settles on his shoulder, instantly reading as a solemn apology at the sacred site.
TEXT: One large single line of Korean brush-calligraphy reading exactly "늦은 사과", energetic Korean brush-calligraphy Hangul lettering, hand-inked strokes, every Hangul jamo drawn complete and correctly formed, bold high-contrast black ink set across the upper sky and kept clear of every face, the only text anywhere in the image.
STYLE: korean webtoon serious drama illustration, bold clean ink lines, dramatic high-contrast shading, intense emotional expression, muted desaturated slate-grey palette.
SCENE: The May 18th National Cemetery memorial tower rises against an overcast Gwangju sky while, in the foreground, an anonymous teenage Korean student in a high-school baseball uniform and cap bows his head deeply in remorse and a dignified elderly Korean education superintendent with short greying hair and glasses rests a wrinkled hand on the boy's bowed shoulder, a few white chrysanthemums resting at their feet on the stone plaza, other uniformed minors standing with lowered heads and anonymous faces in the soft background.
CAMERA: eye-level medium-wide shot centered on the bowed head and the hand on the shoulder as the single focal point, the memorial tower framed behind,

label: 생성
prompt:
GOVERNING: The decisive, instantly recognizable moment — a small cluster of Korean teenage boys in dark school uniforms standing with heads deeply bowed in apology before the tall granite memorial tower of a national democratic-uprising cemetery, a middle-aged man in a dark coat resting his weathered hand on the bowed shoulder of the nearest boy, a few white chrysanthemums laid on the wet stone at their feet.
STYLE: reportage press photograph, natural available light, documentary realism, front-page news immediacy, unposed subjects caught mid-action — not a staged studio portrait or magazine editorial.
SCENE: an open-air memorial plaza in Gwangju, the two great curved granite pillars of the memorial tower rising behind the boys, a second group of local high-school students standing quietly facing them across the plaza, carved memorial names and any distant banners kept as soft, unreadable background texture that reads only as atmosphere, dark rain-damp pavement, bare late-autumn trees along the edges, unmistakably Korean.
CAMERA: 35mm reportage lens, eye-level dropping slightly low, framed from a respectful middle distance at a three-quarter angle so the bowed heads and the consoling hand form the focal knot, moderate shallow depth of field.
LIGHT: flat gray overcast afternoon, soft directionless daylight with no hard shadows, muted desaturated palette of charcoal, slate and cold stone.
